title: Locking wrench pliers
abstract: Embodiments of the current invention relate to a pliers wrench. Optionally, the jaws of the pair of pliers lock to an adjustable gap size and/or the gap between the jaws is aligned with a rotational axis of the pair of pliers. For example, there may be two jaws that adjust symmetrically around the rotational axis. In some embodiments, each jaw is closed toward an opposing jaw by a lever. Optionally, a locking mechanism locks the jaw when it reaches half the gap width from the rotational axis. Optionally, each jaw is closable independently of the other jaw. Optionally, a notch in the jaws forms an angle for clasping a bolt perpendicular to an axis of the device. For example, the notch may have a 120 angle.
claims: Claims WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1. A gripping tool comprising: a rotational axis; a pair of jaws arranged symmetrically around said rotational axis; a single adjustment mechanism simultaneously controlling adjustment of each jaw of said pair of jaws symmetrically around said rotational axis. 2. The gripping tool of claim 1, further comprising a pair of handles wherein each handle of said pair of handles is linked to a separate respective jaw of said pair of jaws and wherein closing said pair of handles towards each other closes said pair of jaws to a closed position and wherein said single adjustment mechanism adjusts a location of said each jaw at said closed position. 3. The gripping tool of claim 2, wherein said pair of handles includes a long handle and a shorter handle. 4. The gripping tool of claim 2, wherein a first handle of said pair of handles is entirely on a first side of said rotational axis and a second handle of said pair of handles is on an opposite side of said rotational axis. 5. The gripping tool of claim 1, further comprising: a separate closing mechanism for each jaw of said pair of jaws; and a single adjustment mechanism linked to said separate closing mechanism of said each jaw thereby simultaneously controlling symmetric adjustment of said each jaw of said pair of jaws. 6. The gripping tool of claim 5, wherein at least one said separate closing mechanism includes a locking mechanism for locking a respective jaw in a closed position. 7. The gripping tool of claim 5, further comprising: a body and wherein said each jaw is attached to said body by a respective rotational pivot. 8. The tool of claim 7, wherein said rotational axis is a longitudinal axis of said body. 9. The gripping tool of claim 7, wherein a respective rotation pivot of a first jaw of said pair of jaws is positioned symmetrically to a respective rotational pivot of a second jaw of said pair of jaws across said rotational axis of said tool. 10. The gripping tool of claim 9, further comprising a rotational mechanism for rotating said body about said rotational axis. 11. The gripping tool of claim 10, wherein said rotational mechanism includes at least one component selected from the group consisting of a ratchet, a fitting symmetric to said rotational axis, a central post and a rotational handle. 12. The gripping tool of claim 7, wherein in a closed position said jaws define a gap and wherein a size of said gap is adjusted by said adjustment mechanism and wherein a central axis of said gap remains stationary with respect to said body for a range of gap sizes. 13. The gripping tool of claim 12, wherein said body includes a rotational axis and wherein said central axis of said gap remains coaxial with said rotational axis for said range of gap sizes. 14. The gripping tool of claim 13, wherein said jaws include a notch having a 120- degree angle configured to hold a hex head bolt with an axis of the bolt coaxial to said gap. 15. The gripping tool of claim 9, wherein in a closed position said jaws define a gap and wherein a size of said gap is adjusted by said adjustment mechanism and wherein a central axis of said gap is coaxial with said rotational axis for a range of gap sizes. 16. The gripping tool of claim 15, wherein said range includes gap sizes from 5 mm to 2 cm. 17. The gripping tool of claim 1, wherein said jaws include a notch having a 120- degree angle. 18. A method of rotating an object comprising: adjusting a locking pair of pliers to lock with a gap between closed jaws of the pair of pliers fitting the object while keeping the gap aligned with a rotational axis of the pair of pliers; closing the jaws onto the object with the object coaxial with respect to said rotational axis of the pair of pliers. 19. The method of claim 18, wherein each jaw of said jaws is linked to a separate handle of a pair of handles and wherein said closing includes closing at least one handle of said pair of handles towards an opposite handle of said pair of handles. 20. The method of claim 18, further comprising: locking said jaws in a closed position. 21. The method of claim 20, wherein said pair of pliers has a rotational axis and wherein in said closed position said pair of jaws are positioned symmetrically around said rotational axis. 22. The method of claim 20, wherein said pair of pliers has a rotational axis and wherein when grasped in said closed position said jaws are positioned symmetrically around said rotational axis. 23. The method of claim 18, further comprising: rotating said pair of pliers and said object about said rotational axis of the pair of pliers. 24. The method of claim 23, further wherein said rotating said pair of pliers and said object about said rotational axis of said pair of pliers also includes rotating said object around a longitudinal axis thereof. 25. The method of claim 18, further comprising: wherein said closing includes rotating at least one jaw of said jaws around a pivot. 26. The method of claim 25, wherein said pair of pliers include a body and said pivot attaches said at least one jaw to said body of said pair of pliers. 27. The method of claim 26, wherein said rotational axis is a longitudinal axis of said body and said rotating includes rotating around said longitudinal axis of said body. 28. The method of claim 18, wherein said pair of pliers include a body and each jaw of said jaws is attached to said body by a respective pivot and where a first said respective pivot is situated symmetrically opposite a second said respective pivot with respect to said rotational axis and wherein said closing includes rotating at least one of said jaws around said respective pivot of said at least one jaw. 29. The method of claim 23, wherein said rotating includes rotating a ratchet. 30. The method of claim 18, wherein said jaws include a notch having a 120 degree angle the method further comprising: holding a hexagonal fitting of said object in said notch.
